item.title: If You Don’t Have The Right To Bodily Autonomy, You Don’t Have Any Rights At All.
item.description: https://twitter.com/EricTopol/status/1524894050964279296 They all but set to steal the right to abortion off the women of America, and that will only be the beginning. People will die because of this decision, forced upon the country by Christian extremists sitting on stolen seats. I know you’re all tired of hearing it, but you need to keep hearing it until it fully sinks in; you need to get off your asses and vote to keep the GQP out of power and give the Democrats a proper majority in the senate, or you’re all fucked.
item.dc:creator: Thelrishlnquistion

item.title: "In this country we pay our bills"

item.title: Good news everybody!
item.dc:creator: huntsnhava40

item.title: I made this for my boyfriend for our anniversary...
item.description:
100% hand made. Wooden Box with glass door.
All the peices are hand cut and painted with watercolor
I used 3d printer filament to hold up the peices off the back.
This is where I started. Needed a mock up to see how large I could work woth the box I made.
Painting the peices. I should have just painted it all togther then cut out the broken peices. But my brain tends to make things complicated
Done purely with watercolor.
Peices all painted and cut out.
The foliage. This was the most pain in the ass part. Those little leaves ... my poor finger was so grumpy from the exact knife. Took over 4 hours to cut it all out. These were also painted in water color.
Starting the set up. You can see here how I used the filament to hold up all the peices.
Video showing the led lights on.
Pup tax. Hope yall enjoyed. Thanks for making it this far. :)
item.dc:creator: sunflowertree

item.title: My Collection of (Stolen) Memes That Make Me Chortle
item.description:
Can't lie it really works.
I don't know how to fix this and I've had to start this post over twice, sorry about the bad crop.
Does the resolution on these suck or is it just me?
I don't
This one has been with me for years
IBS is a real menace
Surprised myself with how many dirty memes I have
item.dc:creator: SlightlyBothered

item.title: Vasectomy

item.dc:creator: KingMammajamma

item.title: I DIY'd a tote bag that transforms into a backpack. I made it for $12 out of upcycled couch leather!
item.description:
Final product shots! I also made a video about how I crammed this project in 2 days https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pvdOvqZkLFA&t=141s
the tote bag configuration
Wearing it in Amsterdam as a crossbody bag
The two straps hook together to form a long crossbody strap
I designed this specifically for a trip to Amsterdam and mostly carried it as a backpack
The backside
I biked around Vondelpark in Amsterdam like this
The backside has a luggage passthrough pouch to make it the ultimate travel bag!
Is it me or does he look like he's glowing here?
K this is the last product shot. Let's get into the DIY!
I started with this paper prototype
I got the leather from Renee, who is helping a furniture company repurpose tonnes of their leather scraps. Thanks to Renee, I'm no longer cutting leather off of random abandoned couches any more! If you'd like to order some, check out her facebook group here: https://www.facebook.com/groups/325491416257150 The leather is super affordable and the scraps are biiig
I flattened out my prototype. I love designs that mostly consist of one continuous piece because I'm an engineer and this feels most efficient
Olfa makes the best rotary cutters!
Punching out some stitching holes in my front pocket
I kept it simple but added the V-cut for just a bit of *flair*
Polishing the edges with a wood slicker and tokonole
I lined all the pockets with this avocado green. Excuse my derpy face
Adding a stitch down the middle to split the pocket into two
I kind of regretted doing this because the pockets ended up being too tight to hold anything other than tickets
Getting ready to assemble the pockets to the main body
Check out my hideous hammering posture
After stitching the pockets on, I added 4 brass rivets for some bling
But in doing so, I accidentally punched a hole through the back of my bag UGH. It's ok though, because the hole ends up being covered
This is a stiffener to give the bottom of the bag some rigidity
Making some lower backpack straps so that the main straps don't have to be too long and unwieldy
These straps are hidden by the luggage passthrough. The elastic bands prevent them from falling out
Time to hammer everything into place on the back
I added an interior pocket because I love pockets and couldn't resist. Since the main body is unlined, I had to be strategic about the pocket placement so that the stitching lines aren't visible from the outside. These stitches are covered by the luggage passthrough flap
Now that all the back stuff is done, I can stitch up the luggage flap
I like adding this X stitch on the bottom of my bags. I think it looks cool and it also secures the stiffener into place
I was stitching with some reaaaally loooong thread
This is the inside
and this is the outside!
Time to fold up the sides
This bottom corner stitch was kind of a pain in the ass but I've stitched through worse situations
I decided to do a double stitch on the sides. Not the best decision considering I was tight on time but I think it looks nice
Added a D ring and some more rivets. This D ring is for the crossbody configuration
I couldn't use my stitching pony so this part took a while and hurt my back. I had to roll out the knots in my back against the wall using a tennis ball the next day
Installing some purse magnets to *slightly* deter thieves
I had to modify my pliers by sticking a protective layer on them because I was worried the plier teeth would ruin the rivet hardware. I used packaging from pepcid
It worked pretty well. Would probably be better if I had a real press fixture though. I think I should spend some time upgrading my tools lol
Here are the backpack straps before stitching
I gave it a good sanding after stitching and before polishing it with tokonole. I was up til 4am on day 2 to finish this bag!
Here's the backpack configuration! It fit me well and was surprisingly comfortable too.
I had the best time on my Amsterdam trip and I'm so tickled that I made this travel bag and buddy to hold all my things! If you wanna see how my trip went, check out my travel vlog. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yn6_ZKdOSVs&t=365s
Can you tell I'm excited about the bag? Thank you for scrolling!! Edit: Thank you for all the love and support. I'm seeing a few questions about whether I have a store/sell my goods. I don't yet, but I'm working on it! If you're interested, please join my email list here and you'll be the first to know https://lingchangdesigns.wixsite.com/mysite You can also DM me here or on instagram @_lingchang or @lingchangleathercrafting
item.dc:creator: Elledawg

item.title: Fancy Feast
item.description: One of my sibling's cats brought them a mouse, but also with a side of mixed greens and I thought that was pretty fancy of him.
item.dc:creator: iamerror101

item.title: Here is big surprise...
item.description: Every time I start getting sympathy for Russian soldiers having to be in Ukraine, who would rather not be there getting killed and shit, I see crap like this, and think, 'you fuck fucking fuckers'. (edit) Thanks to Lemonscentedmeth for a link to an article that covers this. https://www.dailymail.co.uk/news/article-10814183/Russian-troops-boobytrap-10-year-old-girls-PIANO-Horrified-mother-finds-grenade.html
item.dc:creator: friendsofsandwiches

item.title: Here are 20 wholesome things, you deserve them (Day 193)
item.description:
Welcome to the 193rd edition in this series, let's do some good today. Here are 20 wholesome things, you deserve them (Day 192) https://imgur.com/gallery/ef4CV1o Here are 20 wholesome things, you deserve them (Day 191) https://imgur.com/gallery/Cg6iteN Here are 20 wholesome things, you deserve them (Days 181-190) https://imgur.com/a/g3sTR0J Here are 20 wholesome things, you deserve them (Days 171-180) https://imgur.com/a/7OnodoA Here are 20 wholesome things, you deserve them (Days 161-170) https://imgur.com/a/3SfMNfg Here are 20 wholesome things, you deserve them (Days 151-160) https://imgur.com/a/a3npRvx Here are 20 wholesome things, you deserve them (Days 100-150) https://imgur.com/a/rdkioD9 Here are 20 wholesome things, you deserve them (First 100 days) https://imgur.com/a/SGVY2vD
It's true
Be kind to each other
Read that one again
We are now at the halfway point of this post If you haven't been drinking enough water today - here is a friendly reminder to grab a cup now.
My old sponsor used to tell me - You can't think your way into a new way of acting, you have to act your way into a new way of thinking.
Our experiences are far more similar than they are different.
Let them help. ... This week I'm doing a fundraiser to help the Humane Association continue rescuing dogs like The Pucketts. If you would like to help, please check out my link below https://www.classy.org/fundraiser/3793511 ... Have a beautiful day everyone, see you Monday
item.dc:creator: MrPuckett
